answer: The most famous couplet in this ghazal for hopelessness is the opening matla (first couplet): 'Koi ummeed bar nahin aati / Koi soorat nazar nahin aati' ('No hope I have is ever answered / No way ahead can be uncovered'). This is the radif-setting opening verse that gives the entire ghazal its name — the line 'Koi ummeed bar nahin aati' (No hope comes to fruition) is the defining statement of hopelessness in the poem. Found in Diwan-E-Ghalib, chapter 115 (work slug: mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c), paragraph p-92c600 in the translation.
